title: Educational explainers (binary splitting, BBP, NTT) + reusable distributed-verified-map-reduce pattern writeup
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. These explainers turn the project's methods into reusable public-good teaching material: binary splitting, BBP/Bellard extraction, NTT multiplication, plus a reusable 'distributed verified map-reduce' pattern writeup. It depends on release-018.
objective: Write source-cited educational explainers of binary splitting, BBP/Bellard extraction, and NTT multiplication, plus a reusable distributed-verified-map-reduce pattern writeup.
acceptanceCriteria: Clear, source-cited explainers of binary splitting, BBP/Bellard extraction, and NTT multiplication; plus a reusable 'distributed verified map-reduce' pattern writeup; CC-BY-4.0.
output: A document set of educational explainers and the distributed-verified-map-reduce pattern writeup.
